meta.name: Classic Challah (Braided Egg Bread)
meta.subtitle: Soft, slightly sweet • braided loaf • great for sandwiches & French toast • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: Everyday challah-style braided egg bread. Formulated for 1 large loaf or 2 smaller loaves. Includes fresh-milled guidance, honey option, and shaping notes.

yield.notes: Makes 1 large loaf (or 2 small loaves). Typically ~14 slices depending on thickness.

tasteProfile.notes: Tender crumb with a gentle sweetness and a glossy crust from egg wash.

ingredients.label: Flour (bread flour preferred; or all-purpose; or fresh milled wheat flour—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: Bread flour improves structure and braid definition. All-purpose works for a softer loaf.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup. Can substitute honey (see variation).

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (or active dry yeast)
ingredients.notes: About 2 1/4 teaspoons. If using active dry, bloom in warm water first (see steps).

ingredients.label: Warm water (95–105°F / 35–40°C)
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. Hold back 20–30g and add only if dough is dry.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (or light olive oil)
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup. Keeps crumb soft and helps bread stay tender.

steps.title: Optional: bloom active dry yeast (if using)
instructions: If using active dry yeast, stir yeast and a pinch of sugar into the warm water (use water from recipe total).
Rest 5–10 minutes until foamy. If using instant yeast, skip blooming.

steps.title: Mix dough
instructions: In a large bowl (or mixer bowl), combine flour, sugar, salt, and (if using instant) yeast.
Add eggs, oil, and most of the warm water (or the bloomed yeast-water). Hold back ~20–30g water.
Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Add a little more water only if dough is very stiff and dry.

steps.title: Knead until smooth and elastic
instructions: Knead until dough is smooth, elastic, and slightly tacky.
Stand mixer: 6–8 minutes on low to medium-low with dough hook.
By hand: 10–12 minutes kneading.
Dough should pass a basic windowpane test (stretches thin without tearing quickly).

steps.title: First rise (bulk)
instructions: Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and rise until doubled, 60–90 minutes depending on room temperature.

steps.title: Divide and pre-shape
instructions: Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface and gently deflate.
Choose braid style:
• 3-strand braid (easiest): divide into 3 equal pieces.
• 4-strand braid: divide into 4 equal pieces.
• 6-strand braid: divide into 6 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a loose ball, cover, and rest 10 minutes to relax gluten.

steps.title: Roll strands and braid
instructions: Roll each piece into a strand ~14–18 inches (35–45 cm) long, tapered slightly at the ends.
Braid the loaf and pinch ends to seal; tuck ends under for a neat finish.
Place on a parchment-lined baking sheet.

steps.title: Final proof
instructions: Cover loosely and proof until very puffy and the braid holds an indentation when gently pressed, 45–75 minutes.
Avoid over-proofing (loaf can flatten and lose definition).

steps.title: Egg wash and bake
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Optional: whisk egg wash (1 egg + water/milk) and brush gently over loaf; add sesame or poppy seeds if using.
Bake 28–38 minutes until deep golden brown.
Internal temp target: 190–200°F (88–93°C). If browning too quickly, tent loosely with foil for the last 10 minutes.

steps.title: Cool before slicing
instructions: Cool on a rack at least 45 minutes before slicing so crumb sets.
Challah slices beautifully once fully cooled and is excellent toasted the next day.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour can make challah denser if used 100% unsifted. Blending and longer rest improve softness and braid definition.
guidelines: Best texture for braids: use 70% fresh milled flour + 30% bread flour (same total flour weight).
If using 100% fresh milled (unsifted), consider sifting out some bran; expect slightly tighter crumb and slightly shorter strands.
Fresh milled flour may need +10–30g extra water. Add only if dough feels stiff after mixing and kneading begins.
An extra 10–15 minute rest after mixing (before kneading) can improve extensibility (autolyse-style rest).

variations.name: Honey challah (classic sweetness)
variations.change: Replace sugar with 60g honey. Reduce water by ~15g (honey adds moisture).

variations.name: Two smaller loaves
variations.change: After bulk rise, divide dough in half and braid two loaves. Bake 22–28 minutes.

variations.name: Raisin challah
variations.change: Add 120g raisins during the last minute of kneading (or fold in by hand).

variations.name: Seeded crust
variations.change: After egg wash, apply sesame, poppy, or everything-seasoning blend.

storage.counter: Store airtight up to 3 days. Keeps best wrapped well.
storage.freezer: Freeze whole loaf or slices up to 2 months. Thaw at room temp; toast slices to refresh.
storage.reheat: Toast slices or warm whole loaf briefly in a low oven (300°F / 150°C) wrapped in foil.

notes: For a taller, more defined braid: keep strands even in thickness and avoid over-proofing.
If strands shrink back while rolling: cover and rest 5–10 minutes, then continue.
Challah is excellent for French toast—use slices 1–2 days old for best soak without collapse.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions.
